text:
Saudi Arabia - UAR: The recent appearance of a new UAR military mission in Saudi Arabia probably reflects an attempt by Faysal to implement a gradual improvement in Saudi-UAR relations. The chief of the United States military advisory group in Saudi Arabia is concerned that the UAR training mission may attempt to discredit American advisory assistance.
Saudi consideration of curtailment of at least some of the American military advisory activities is suggested in an early July message asking the maintenance branches at two major army posts to report on the number of American advisers, how much they were needed, and the possibility of getting along without them

text:
USSR: A valid countdown began at the Tyura Tam Missile Test Range at 0200Z 25 July (2200 EDT 24 July). The countdown was proceeding on schedule as of 0200 EDT 25 July.
Barring delays in the countdown the launching should take place about 1000Z 25 July (0600 EDT). Present indications are that the launching will involve a test ICBM rather than a space vehicle. Twelve successful test ICBMs have been launched to date, six of them in 1959
